<<<KOT:oocyte>>>>
{"kind":"cannot-formalise","why":"An oocyte is a specialized cell type defined by its role in mammalian sexual reproduction and its developmental stage within oogenesis. Its identity conditions depend on technical embryology (primary vs. secondary oocyte distinctions, meiotic arrest stages, chromosomal ploidy) and cellular biology (organelle ultrastructure, gene expression patterns) that cannot be grounded in the prime lexicon or the current catalog. It requires the biology/medicine sector, not the everyday perception tier."}
<<<END:oocyte>>>>

<<<KOT:palaeoanthropology>>>>
{"kind":"cannot-formalise","why":"Palaeoanthropology is a formal academic discipline that studies human evolutionary origins through fossil evidence, anatomical reconstruction, and kinship inference within Hominidae. It is defined by its specialized methods (phylogenetic inference, comparative morphology, stratigraphic dating), theoretical commitments (evolutionary taxonomy), and domain-specific artifacts (fossil skulls, stone tools interpreted as cultural evidence). These cannot be formalized in the prime lexicon without introducing discipline-specific concepts that lie outside everyday perception and the kernel catalog."}
<<<END:palaeoanthropology>>>>
